item.name: ALL
item.description: Special item used for GET/TAKE ALL

item.name: LIFT
item.description: Used as noun for LIFT BUTTON but it's not exactly a synonym for BUTTON

item.name: LOCK
item.description: Used noun for locks on doors, desks etc but not really a synonym for DESK is it

item.name: STONE
item.description: Syonym for ROCK

item.name: LAMPPOST
item.description: Synonym for LAMP

item.name: LANTERN
item.description: Synonym for LAMP

item.name: MINITRUE PASS
item.description: A pass into the Ministry of Truth

item.name: TELESCREEN
item.description: It's playing brassy music. There's a slot on the side and a camera above the main screen. It is securely fixed to the wall.

item.name: MINIPAX PASS
item.description: A pass into the Ministry of Peace

item.name: MINIPLENTY PASS
item.description: A pass into the Ministry of Plenty

item.name: DESK
item.description: It fits neatly in an alcove out of sight of the Telescreen so it seems the drawer could be used to store contraband.

item.name: OPEN DESK
item.description: The drawer is open.
item.prefix: In the drawer of the desk is

item.name: DIARY
item.description:
The pages are of a beautiful creamy coloured paper. It's mostly blank but on the first page you have made an entry:
April 4th, 1984.
"If there is hope, it lies in the proles."

item.name: INKPEN
item.description: Requires ink to be of use.

item.name: BLOCKED SINK
item.description: The water is brown and reeks of cabbage.

item.name: SINK
item.description: Nothing special.

item.name: PLUNGER
item.description: For unblocking your sink with.

item.name: DESK
item.description: It is strewn with bits of discarded electronics and burned out circuit boards. It has a drawer.

item.name: OPEN DESK
item.description: It is strewn with bits of discarded electronics and burned out circuit boards.
item.prefix: In the drawer of the desk is

item.name: CARETAKERS KEY
item.description: A heavy, iron key stamped with the flats' maintenance insignia.

item.name: SERVICE DOOR
item.description: A heavy steel door stamped with the flats' maintenance insignia.

item.name: SERVICE DOOR
item.description: A heavy steel door stamped with the flats' maintenance insignia. Its hinges are well maintained and swings open with ease.

item.name: RUSTY KEY
item.description: A rusty metal key.

item.name: SHORTING WIRE
item.description: A length of stripped copper wire with scorched insulation. It looks like it was pulled from discarded electrical equipment. It might be used to short out a component.

item.name: LOOSE ROCK
item.description: A fist-sized lump of the ruined fountain, heavy enough to throw.

item.name: LAMP
item.description: The glass will definitely fall if struck with something. You're surprised nobody has broken it already.

item.name: SERVICE GRATE
item.description: A rusted iron grate set into the ground. It looks like it could jemmied open, but it is either locked or rusted tight.

item.name: BROKEN GLASS
item.description: Shards of glass from the broken lamp.

item.name: BLANK PCB
item.description: It has a telescreen connector but the board is otherwise blank except for the CPU contact frame.

item.name: COMPONENTS
item.description: Resistors, capacitors and integrated circuits required for building a board.

item.name: SOLDERING IRON
item.description: It's small enough to hide in your overalls.

item.name: CROWBAR
item.description: A solid iron crowbar. A bit rusty but it might still useful for forcing a lock.
item.onMiss: You swing the crowbar at :1 but it fails to connect.

item.name: CHILDS DOLL
item.description: A mouldering child's doll. Stuffed with sawdust, missing one button eye.

item.name: PRESERVED FOOD
item.description: A tin of luncheon meat. These don't come in the party rations.

item.name: FLASK OF GIN
item.description: London Gin. This is the real deal, much higher quality than Victory Gin.

item.name: SACK OF SUGAR
item.description: A small cloth sack full of sugar. This is obviously contraband.

item.name: COFFEE
item.description: It has a rich aroma; this is definitely Inner Party grade.

item.name: DRIED BEANS
item.description: A small sack of dried pulses. Difficult to cook but it's more food than the Party would provide in a month.

item.name: SCRAP METAL
item.description: Scrap but you might be able to trade it at market.

item.name: ETCHING ACID
item.desciption: A small jar of acid ith a rubber stopper. It is very dangerous.

item.name: BOARD GAME
item.description: Square ivory tiles carved with a black ebony letter and a smaller number inlaid on each one. All the tiles are kept in a green baize cloth bag. The board is of maple or similar hardwood and has curious coloured squares in a star pattern proclaiming double points and triple points.

item.name: ARMCHAIR
item.description: A moth-eaten armchair.
item.prefix: Resting on the seat of the armchair is

item.name: LEAFLET
item.description: A crudely printed leaflet, half smudged with damp. Someone has scrawled a time and place on the back in a shaky hand. The words read: Meeting - Victory Square. Ask for Silas. It looks like a secret invitation rather than Party-approved propaganda.

item.name: CIGARETTES
item.description: A small pack of cigarettes - not Victory-brand. These will fetch a good price with the right buyer.

item.name: BINOCULARS
item.description: A pair of battered lenses fixed to a wooden frame. They don't give great magnification, but you can pick out distant figures and roofs.

item.name: CONTRABAND BOOK
item.description: The Theory and Practice of Oligarchical Collectivism by Emmanuel Goldstein.

item.name: VIDEO CPU
item.description: Can be used for decoding network signals and displaying video output.

item.name: CIRCUIT STENCIL
item.description: A pattern of traces and pads marking out an intricate schematic. Required components are marked out with reference designators and part numbers.

item.name: ASSEMBLED BOARD
item.description: An assembled circuit board for plugging into a Telescreen. It has a connector along one side that snugly fits into a Telescreen adaptor. There's a bus alongside one of the chips used to connect some sort of input device.

item.name: LOCKPICK
item.description: A small intricate looking device, thin steel levers with various shaped tips for manipulating the internal components of a lock.

item.name: KITCHEN CUPBOARD
item.description: It is clearly aging and the paint is peeling but it's a serviceable cupboard.

item.name: LIFT BUTTON
item.description: A black plastic button, grimy with dirt from thousands of past uses.

item.name: OBSOLETE TREATY
item.description: A thick bound document stamped OBSOLETE in red ink. The pages are densely typed, the language precise and absolute and yet several sections are marked for revision, others simply crossed out. You have the distinct sense this was never meant to be read again.

item.name: SONG SHEET
item.description:
A thin sheet of cheap paper intended for children. A simple illustration shows a smiling spider climbing a vertical pipe beneath a bright Party emblem.
Incy Wincy Spider climbed the social flue, With fervent Party zeal and purpose always true. Sudden came the Thoughtcrime, an icy chilling rain, And Incy Wincy Spider was never seen again.

item.name: SECURITY HATCH
item.description: A hatch of heavy steel bars with a simple sliding bolt to close it, easily opened from one side but unreachable from the other.

item.name: DISPOSAL CHUTE
item.description: A circular metal opening set into the wall. Warm air rises steadily from below. Far below, something roars. Paper flutters briefly, then vanishes into the heat. Nothing returns from the memory hole.

item.name: FILING CABINET
item.description: A tall steel cabinet, the drawers are labelled with dates, districts, and numeric codes. The drawers are locked with simple fragile looking locks.

item.name: FILING CABINET
item.description: The cabinet drawer is open, revealing neatly filed photograph folders.

item.name: PHOTO LEDGER
item.description: A bound ledger recording image alterations and reassignments. Inside are hundreds of photographs. Faces often repeat but with different names. Some names are crossed out entirely, replaced by numbers. Several pages reference transfers to Records - Room 26.

item.name: DOCUMENT TRAY
item.description: Stacked trays labelled INCOMING, REVISION, APPROVED, DISPOSAL. Each tray contains sheaves of greyish paper. You flick through several sheets: A speech praising increased rations becomes one announcing a further increase while the figures clearly show a decrease. A newspaper article marked 'refs unpersons' with names changed. Several photos stapled to one sheet show the same scene but with different people.

item.name: DESK
item.description: A heavy metal desk with a single locked drawer.

item.name: OPEN DESK
item.prefix: In the drawer of the desk is
item.description: A heavy metal desk with a deep drawer.

item.name: LEATHER FOLDER
item.description: A neatly organised folder stamped INTERNAL. It contains procedural memos and transfer authorisations. The documents describe correction procedures, prisoner transfers, and photographic verification. Records are consolidated in a single secure archive in Records Room 26.

item.name: TELESCREEN
item.description: The screen is active, though silent. The glass reflects you too clearly.

item.name: RUBBER STAMPS
item.description: A pair of stamps. One stamps AUTHORIZED, the other UNAUTHORIZED.
